rulings.Case Title: FELIX BRAYNIN et al VS. VLADISLAV CHERNOGUZ et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 17, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANTS VLADISLAV CHERNOGUZ, BIANA CHERNOGUZ' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendants failed to shift the burden. Defendants have not established that the parties entered into a binding settlement agreement on March 27, 2012. Defendants' owm evidence shows that Defendants' counsel intended to forward a "revised draft" of the agreement to Plaintiffs on that date. (Ex. 40.) Exhibits G, H and I (Freeman Declaration) show that the parties continued to negotiate the terms of the settlement after March 27, 2012. Defendants also fail to establish as a matter of law that plaintiffs are estopped from denying the existence of the settlement. Defendants fail to show that they reasonably relied on the alleged representations. Defendant Vladislav Chernoguz admits at 13:14-21 of his deposition that he, too, believed that an executed written agreement was necessary to settle the action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ALLIED HEATING & AIR CONDITIONING, INC., A VS. GOTHAM BAY CONSTRUCTION CORPORATION, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Complaint (#521524)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 17, 2013, Line 9, DEFENDANT BRANDO EMPIRE SAN FRANCISCO, LLC, BRANDY HO, JIMMY LAM'S Notice Of Motion To Strike Complaint (#521524) The motion to strike attorney's fees is (1) granted with leave to amend as to the breach of written contract cause of action. Plaintiff has leave to allege, if it can do so in good faith, that the parties entered into the written contract in light of the lack of defendants' signature and par. 14.1.1; (2) granted without leave to amend as to causes of action 2, 4, and 5. Plaintiff concedes that those claims do not support the relief; (3) denied as moot as to the 3rd cause of action based on the demurrer ruling; and (4) denied as to the breach of oral contract cause of action. Plaintiff alleges that the parties orally agreed to the terms of the written contract. The Court needs to take those allegations as true and the Court cannot determine whether the parties actually entered into an oral contract and what the terms of that contract are on a motion to strike. The motion to strike Exhibits I, N and K is granted. The checks are evidentiary in nature; Plaintiff needs only to prove ultimate facts and does not need to attach unredacted checks with bank account numbers. The checks are "improper" within the meaning of CCP 435 and 436. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ARROWOOD SURPLUS LINES INSURANCE COMPANY VS. CALIBER ONE INSURANCE COMPANY, NOW KNOWN AS MAXUM et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 17, 2013, Line 10, CROSS DEFENDANT ACCEPTANCE INSURANCE COMPANY'S Mtn To Dismiss The motion to dismiss or, in the alternative, stay proceedings is denied. AIC has been in rehabilitation in Nebraska for five years. Even if Nebraska is a reciprocal state under the UILA for purposes of California law, dismissal or stay of this particular action is not warranted. The UILA does not prohibit a party from maintaining a California action to obtain a judgment against an insurer that is in insolvency or rehabilitation proceedings elsewhere. (See Hawthorne Sav. F.S.B. v. Reliance Ins. Co. of Ill. 421 F. 3d 835, 855-56 (9th Cir. 2005.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: KARMA QUICK VS. JONATHAN FRIED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 17, 2013, Line 3: PLAINTIFF KARMA QUICK'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses and Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. DEFENDANT, JONATHAN FRIED'S d/b/a LAW OFFICES OF JONATHAN FRIED ("FRIED") IS ORDERED TO FILE RESPONSIVE ANSWERS TO EACH SUBPART OF THE FOLLOWING FORM INTERROGATORIES: NO. 200.2, 200.6, 201.1, 201.2, 201.3, 201.4, 201.5, 201.6, 206.2, 206.3, 207.1, 207.2, 209.2, 211.2, 211.3, 214.1, 214.2, 215.1, 215.2 & 217.1. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS DENIED TO ALL OTHER FORM INTERROGATORIES. ALTHOUGH THE FORM INTERROGATORIES DEFINES "EMPLOYMENT" WHERE AN EMPLOYEE PROVIDES SERVICES OTHER THAN AS AN INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR, THE FORM INTERROGATORIES DEFINES "EMPLOYEE" AS KARMA QUICK AND "EMPLOYER" AS JONATHAN FRIED. THE INFORMATION SOUGHT IN THESE FORM INTERROGATORIES ARE IN MANY CASES RELEVANT TO THE ISSUE AS TO WHETHER OR NOT PLAINTIFF WAS AN INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR AS CLAIMED BY DEFENDANT AND SHE IS ENTITLED TO RESPONSES TO THOSE INTERROGATORIES. CALIFORNIA ADOPTED FORM INTERROGATORIES TO SIMPLIFY DISCOVERY RATHER THAN REQUIRING A PLAINTIFF IN A CASE WHERE THE EMPLOYER SEEKS TO CONTEST THE EMPLOYMENT RELATIONSHIP TO PROPOUND A SPECIAL SET OF INTERROGATORIES TO SEEK INFORMATION THAT IS RELEVANT IF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM OF AN EMPLOYMENT RELATIONSHIP IS UPHELD. AS TO THE RESPONSE TO 217.1 WHEREIN. DEFENDANT SIMPLY MADE REFERENCE TO DOCUMENTS, THAT IS INSUFFICIENT TO RESPOND TO THE SPECIFIC REQUESTS SUBPARAGRAPHS (a) - (d). DEFENDANT SHOULD EITHER ANSWER DIRECTLY OR POINT TO WHERE IN THE DOCUMENTS THE ANSWERS MAY BE FOUND. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED AS THE POSITION TAKEN BY DEFENDANT IS NOT WITHOUT SOME JUSTIFICATION GIVEN THE LANGUAGE OF THE FORM INTERROGATORIES. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL WILL PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT IT TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER A COPY OF THE PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON APRIL 17, 2017. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JANET HAAS VS. CHRISTOPHER I. MCCOLE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order; And Staying The Deposition And Quashing The Deposition Notice And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 17, 2013, Line 4: PLAINTIFF JANET HAAS' Motion For Protective Order; And Staying The Deposition And Quashing The Deposition Notice And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER QUASHING THE NOTICE OF HER DEPOSITION AND STAYING THE DEPOSITION FOR 60 DAYS IS GRANTED, ALL DISCOVERY WILL BE STAYED FOR 60 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. THERE HAS BEEN NO SHOWING BY DEFENDANT THAT THERE IS AN IMMEDIATE NEED FOR DISCOVERY TO GO FORWARD AT THIS TIME AND PLAINTIFF'S SHOWING OF GOOD CAUSE FOR DELAY IS SUFFICIENT TO JUSTIFY THE STAY. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED. rulings.Case Title: DILLINGHAM & MURPHY, LLP VS. RAILROAD TECHNOLOGY CORPORATION , A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 17, 2013, Line 16, DEFENDANT RAILROAD TECHNOLOGY CORPORATION, ROBERT NESS' MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS The motion to quash service of summons is denied. Plaintiff has produced facts showing Defendants Ness and Railroad Technology Corporation have California contacts relevant to this case. Among other facts, Plaintiff has shown that the retainer agreement was entered into in California; services on behalf of Defendants were performed in California; Mr. Ness was a resident in California when the cause of action arose; and Ness and RTC conducted business in California. Defendants have not met their burden of staying or dismissing this case for forum non conveniens. Balancing the public and private factors, California has a far greater interest in hearing this matter than Florida. See Stangvik v. Shiley Inc. (1991) 54 Cal. App. 3d 744. Most of the events took place in California; no events took place in Florida. Plaintiff's records and timekeepers are in California. The case involves a dispute relating to the Bar and recovery of attorney's fees; Florida has no interest in such a lawsuit. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, April 17, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANT EDNA VIERYA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT This motion is off calendar as moot. Plaintiff filed First Amended Complaint on March 27, 2013. =(302/MJM)
